pipeline.testNotes: Verification complete. All quality gates passed: typecheck (✓), lint (✓), test suite (716 test files, 7399 tests ✓). Browser UAT: (1) LegacyImportDialog component wired in SettingsPage.tsx line 4176, (2) Button 'Import conversations from old Panopticon' visible in Experimental section (line 4157), (3) Dialog structure verified: role='dialog', checkboxes with alreadyImported disabled state, Select-all/Select-none controls (lines 235,246), N/M counter (line 249), (4) GET /api/settings/legacy-import/conversations endpoint returns preview data with conversations array, hasFavorite, claudeSessionId, alreadyImported fields. (5) Component tree verified: checkboxes, summary on import complete. All 5 beads complete, feature fully implemented and tested.
pipeline.verificationNotes:
Acceptance criteria check FAILED — 13/13 AC incomplete:

### Acquire the restart lock before the pan reload dev-marker SIGUSR2 branch (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given a `pan dev` marker is present AND the restart lock is already held, when `pan reload` runs, then it records success=false (trigger='pan reload'), does NOT call process.kill(...SIGUSR2), and exits with code 2.
  - [ ] Given a `pan dev` marker is present AND the lock is free, when `pan reload` runs, then it acquires the lock, sends SIGUSR2 to the dev pid, records success=true, and releases the lock (existing PAN-1662 test stays green and additionally asserts acquireRestartLock was called).
  - [ ] Given no dev marker and the lock is held by another process, when `pan reload` runs, then it records success=false 'restart in progress' and exits 2 without running bun install or the build (existing detached-path behavior preserved).

### Append every restart-status write to an append-only restart-events.jsonl journal (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given two writeRestartStatus calls in sequence, when the journal is read with readRestartEvents(), then it returns BOTH entries in order while restart-status.json still contains only the last entry.
  - [ ] Given two successful entries 2.6s apart with different pids, when detectConcurrentRestartWriters() runs, then it returns both entries; given the same pid or entries >60s apart, then it returns an empty array.
  - [ ] Given the journal already holds 200 lines, when another entry is appended, then the file is trimmed to the most recent 200 entries.
  - [ ] Given the journal path is unwritable, when writeRestartStatus runs, then restart-status.json is still written successfully and no error propagates to the caller.

### Surface the restart writer (pid + initiator) and concurrent-writer warning in pan status (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given a restart-status with pid=1733121 and initiator='conv-20260610-573d', when `pan status` renders, then the restart line includes 'pid 1733121' and the initiator.
  - [ ] Given the journal contains two successful pan reload entries 2.6s apart with different pids, when formatRestartStatusLines runs, then it emits a warning line naming both pids.
  - [ ] Given no concurrent writers in the journal, when formatRestartStatusLines runs, then no warning line is emitted and the existing single-line format is preserved.

### Supervisor stop waits for exit; start verifies survival — no dead pidfile (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given a live supervisor pid, when stopSupervisorProcessSync runs, then it does not unlink the pidfile or return until isProcessAlive(pid) is false (or the SIGKILL-escalation bound elapses).
  - [ ] Given startSupervisorProcessSync spawns a child that dies immediately, when start completes, then it logs an error and leaves NO pidfile pointing at the dead pid.
  - [ ] Given the bounded sync waits, when reviewed, then they use Atomics.wait (not a busy-loop and not execSync) and total stop-wait never exceeds ~2s.
